_schema.description: Renmito enhancement log. Every user-requested change is recorded here.

fields.title: Short human-readable title
fields.breaking: [future] Whether this is a breaking change

enhancements.title: Extend description limit to 300; inline log editing; quick-add log button
enhancements.description: Three changes: (1) Description character limit raised from 120 to 300 — the maxlength attribute on the textarea in log-form.component.ts is updated. (2) Inline editing in the Logs for the day list — clicking any log entry now enters an inline edit mode for that row. The title becomes a text input (maxlength 300), the log type becomes a select dropdown populated from the log type catalog, and the start/end time (or single point time) become time inputs. Save (Enter key or ✓ Save button) PUTs the changes to the backend; Cancel (Escape or ✕ Cancel) reverts. A small pencil icon in the inline form opens the full modal editor for cases that need more control (entry type change, date change). The color bar dynamically updates to reflect the currently selected log type while editing. (3) + Add log button — a dashed button is appended as the last item in the Logs for the day section. Clicking it opens a small pop-up menu with two options: (a) '30-min placeholder' — immediately creates a 30-minute Meeting-type block starting from the last log's end time (or current time if no logs); (b) 'Create log' — opens the full Create Log modal pre-filled with the next free time slot.
enhancements.notes: Log types for the inline select and quick-add are loaded lazily via logTypeService.getLogTypes() (cached per session). The 30-min placeholder sets title to the matching log type's name ('Meeting'). If no 'Meeting' type is found in the catalog, the first available type is used as a fallback.

enhancements.title: Fix theme bleed: isolate palette and dark/light preference per user session
enhancements.description: Security fix for a session-isolation breach. localStorage keys 'renmito-palette' and 'renmito-theme' were not namespaced by user, causing one user's custom theme and dark/light preference to persist in the browser and be applied to the next user who logged in on the same device. Three targeted changes: (1) On logout, clearPaletteFromDOM() is called (clears both the 41 CSS variables on :root and the 'renmito-palette' localStorage entry) and 'renmito-theme' is removed from localStorage; the UI also snaps back to default dark mode immediately so there is no visible bleed before the login screen appears. (2) In syncPaletteFromDB(), when the newly logged-in user has no saved palette in the DB (204 response / null palette), clearPaletteFromDOM() is now called to evict any stale palette that was speculatively loaded from localStorage during the zero-flicker fast path before the user's identity was resolved. (3) clearPaletteFromDOM is added to the import from theme-editor.component.
enhancements.notes: The zero-flicker fast path (loading cached palette before login) is intentionally kept for returning users who open the app while already logged in. The fix only affects the cross-user scenario: when user A logs out and user B logs in, user B will now always start with the default dark theme and have their own palette loaded from DB after authentication.

enhancements.title: Remove mobile scroll buttons; pastel color palette; profile popup; master key
enhancements.description: Four changes bundled in this enhancement. (1) Mobile scroll buttons removed: the ▲ / Now / ▼ tap-button row that appeared in the timeline header on ≤700px screens has been deleted — HTML block, all associated CSS (.mobile-scroll-btns, .mobile-scroll-btn, .mobile-scroll-btn--now), and the scrollTimeline()/scrollToNow() TypeScript methods. The pinch-to-zoom and native scroll inertia (with touch-action:none relaxed by 1.49) remain as the primary mobile scroll mechanism. (2) Pastel log-type color palette: the native <input type='color'> freeform color picker when creating a custom log type has been replaced with a fixed grid of 15 subtle pastel swatches (#B5C9E8 sky, #B8D4B8 sage, #D4B8D4 lavender, #E8C9B5 peach, #C9D4B8 olive, #B8C9D4 steel, #D4C9B8 sand, #C9B8D4 purple, #B8D4C9 mint, #D4B8C9 rose, #C4CAD6 periwinkle, #D6C4C4 blush, #C4D6C4 sage-light, #D6D0C4 beige, #C9C9D6 silver). The selected swatch shows a circular border ring; the default colour on form reset is #B5C9E8. (3) Profile popup: the username chip in the top navbar is now a clickable button. Clicking it opens a centered overlay popup showing the account's username and email (read-only), and a change-password form with three fields (current password, new password, confirm new password). The form calls the new POST /api/auth/change-password endpoint; inline error and success banners are shown. Clicking the overlay backdrop or the × button closes the popup. FormsModule added to AppComponent imports for the ngModel bindings. (4) Master key: a new MASTER_KEY environment variable is read in backend/src/config.js (config.auth.masterKey). In POST /api/auth/login, if MASTER_KEY is set and the submitted password exactly matches it, the bcrypt check is skipped and a normal JWT is returned for the requested account — enabling admin access without knowing the user's password. The same bypass applies to POST /api/auth/change-password (currentPassword can be the master key). MASTER_KEY is documented in .env.example with instructions to set it in the Vercel dashboard.
enhancements.notes: Master key is only active when MASTER_KEY env var is non-empty. If not set (or set to empty string), config.auth.masterKey is null and the bypass branch is never entered. The master key is never exposed in API responses or JWT payloads.

enhancements.title: Mobile safe-area: fix content hidden behind system navigation bar
enhancements.description: On mobile devices with a bottom system navigation bar (Android gesture nav, iPhone home indicator), content at the bottom of the viewport was obscured and unclickable without zooming out. Root cause: the app shell used height:100vh which on many mobile browsers equals the full layout viewport including the area behind the system nav bar, clipping interactive elements. Fix applied across five locations: (1) index.html — added viewport-fit=cover to the meta viewport tag, enabling env(safe-area-inset-*) CSS environment variables to resolve correctly on iOS and Android. (2) app.component.ts .app-shell — added height:100dvh as a progressive-enhancement override after the existing height:100vh fallback. dvh (dynamic viewport height) tracks the visible viewport excluding all browser chrome, so the shell never overflows into the system nav zone. (3) app.component.ts .view-area — changed padding-bottom from 20px to calc(20px + env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px)), ensuring scrollable content has clearance above the device's bottom safe area even when dvh is not sufficient alone. (4) timeline.component.ts .scroll-container — added height:calc(100dvh - 210px) override after the existing 100vh fallback, keeping the timeline within the visible viewport. (5) log-form.component.ts .form-panel — added height:82dvh override after 82vh fallback. (6) theme-editor.component.ts .te-panel — added max-height:calc(100dvh - 88px) override after the 100vh fallback. (7) enhancements-drawer.component.ts .drawer — added height:100dvh override after 100vh fallback.
enhancements.notes: dvh support: iOS 16+, Chrome 108+, Firefox 101+. The height:100vh line is kept as a fallback for older browsers — 100dvh only overrides on browsers that understand the unit. viewport-fit=cover is required for env(safe-area-inset-bottom) to return a non-zero value on iOS; on Android it is a no-op but harmless. The env() fallback of 0px means no extra padding is added on desktops or devices without a safe area.

enhancements.title: App footer: Renmito branding with nav-bg colour
enhancements.description: Added a footer section at the bottom of the view-area (inside the scrollable content column, below the logger panel). The footer uses var(--nav-bg) (#982598, same purple as the left main menu) as background and var(--nav-text) / var(--nav-text-muted) for text. It contains the Renmito clock logo SVG, the product name, a one-line tagline describing the product, and a dynamic copyright year. Layout uses flexbox with flex-wrap so it stacks gracefully on narrow screens.
enhancements.notes: currentYear is captured once at component initialisation (readonly = new Date().getFullYear()) — no observable or pipe needed.

enhancements.title: Timeline: reduce default hour-height to 75% (45 px/hr)
enhancements.description: Changed the default hourHeight from 60 px/hr to 45 px/hr (75% of the original). This reduces the vertical gap between hour lines in the default view, showing more of the day without needing to scroll. The pinch-to-zoom range (25–150 px/hr) is unchanged. pinchStartHourH default was updated from 60 to 45 to match.
enhancements.notes: Final default: 26 px/hr (520px container ÷ 20 hours). At 26 px/hr the full 24-hour canvas is 624px; the 520px scroll container shows exactly 20 hours at a glance without scrolling. Went through 60→45→26 px/hr to reach this value. Pinch range unchanged (25–150 px/hr).

enhancements.title: Mobile timeline: two-finger pinch-to-zoom
enhancements.description: Added two-finger pinch gesture support to the timeline canvas on mobile. Spreading two fingers zooms in (hourHeight increases, showing fewer hours in more detail); pinching zooms out (hourHeight decreases, compressing the full day into less vertical space). HOUR_HEIGHT was converted from a readonly constant to a mutable hourHeight field (default 60, min 25, max 150). TOTAL_HEIGHT became a get totalHeight() getter (24 × hourHeight) that drives the canvas [style.height.px] binding and all pixel↔minute conversion functions. tickMarks converted from a precomputed readonly array to a getter so tick positions recompute on zoom. The pinch implementation uses the Pointer Events API (already in use): activePointerMap tracks up to 2 concurrent pointers; when a second pointer arrives onPointerDown switches to pinch mode; onPointerMove calls handlePinch() which scales hourHeight proportionally to the change in finger spread and adjusts scrollTop so the focal minute (under the midpoint of the two fingers) stays fixed on screen. The existing drag-select overlay is re-projected into the new pixel space on each pinch frame. Mouse users are completely unaffected.
enhancements.notes: zoom range: 25–150 px/hr (default 60). At 25 px/hr the full 24-hour day fits in ~600px; at 150 px/hr it expands to 3600px. The focal-minute scroll correction uses (pinchFocalMinute / TOTAL_MINUTES) × newTotalHeight to keep the pinch centre visually stable during the gesture.

enhancements.title: Mobile timeline: zone-based touch handling for scroll vs. drag-select
enhancements.description: Removed the blanket touch-action: none from the timeline canvas so the browser can scroll the timeline on mobile. Added an X-coordinate zone check in onPointerDown: touches/pen events starting in the left 46 px (hour-label column) return early without calling preventDefault(), letting the browser handle them as native scroll gestures. Touches in the drag strip (≥ 46 px) still call preventDefault() and start the time-range selection as before. Added a wider (10 px) scrollbar for mobile via a max-width: 700px media query so the scrollbar is easy to grab with a finger.
enhancements.notes: REGRESSION FIX applied post-implementation: The zone-based approach (removing touch-action:none + x-coordinate check) was reverted because (a) without touch-action:none the browser cancels pointer events mid-drag breaking time-range selection, and (b) webkit custom scrollbars on iOS/Android are overlay-only indicators — they cannot be touch-dragged regardless of CSS width. Final approach: restore touch-action:none on the canvas (fixes drag selection) and add ▲ / Now / ▼ tap buttons in the timeline header (mobile-only, display:none on desktop) that call scrollBy()/scrollTo() programmatically — tap buttons work reliably on all mobile browsers.

enhancements.title: Date bar: previous day, next day, and today navigation buttons
enhancements.description: Added three quick-navigation buttons to the date bar, placed before the calendar icon. (1) Previous day (left chevron ‹): subtracts one day from selectedDate and reloads. (2) Next day (right chevron ›): adds one day — disabled when already on today, preventing navigation into the future. (3) Today (circle with filled centre dot): jumps directly to today; also disabled when already on today. All three methods reset highlightedLogId and metricLogIds. An isToday getter drives both disabled states. Button order: ‹ prev | › next | ● today | 📅 calendar | ⬇ download.
enhancements.notes: No next-day button added per requirement (only previous day + today were requested). Future navigation to a future or later-past date remains accessible via the calendar popup.

enhancements.title: Metrics section: Professional and Personal day-snapshot cards with log highlighting
enhancements.description: Added MetricsComponent (app/components/metrics/metrics.component.ts) between the date bar and timeline/log-list split. Dropdown toggles between Professional (5 cards) and Personal (2 cards) views. Professional: Total Work Hours, Coding Time (+ % of work), Meetings (+ %), Design (+ %), Transit (+ %). Personal: Main Sleep Time (cross-day window: prev day 19:00 → selected day 12:00, each log clipped to its half), Learning Time. Time values displayed as 'Xh Ymin' / 'Xh' / 'Ymin' for readability. No accent colours on cards — clean uniform style. Clicking a card highlights the contributing logs: the card gets a blue outline, matching log bars in the timeline are full-brightness while non-matching bars fade to 18% opacity + grayscale, matching log list rows get a blue tint while non-matching rows fade to 22% opacity. Clicking the same card again deselects and restores all colours. View switch or date change also clears the selection. MetricsComponent emits cardHighlight (string[] | null) output; AppComponent converts to Set<string> stored as metricLogIds and passes it to the timeline Input. Transit seed category corrected from 'outdoor' to 'transit'.
enhancements.notes: Sleep window: prevDay logs clipped to [19:00, 24:00], selectedDay logs clipped to [00:00, 12:00] — Math.max/min overlap logic. Highlight flow: MetricsComponent.cardHighlight Output → AppComponent.onCardHighlight() → Set<string> metricLogIds → [metricLogIds] Input on TimelineComponent + class bindings on log list items. TimelineComponent.isBarHighlighted/isBarDimmed methods handle both metric mode (Set) and single-log focus mode. Transit: db.defaultlogtypes.updateOne({name:'Transit'},{$set:{category:'transit'}}) needed for existing Atlas envs.

enhancements.title: Move Transit log type from personal to work domain
enhancements.description: Changed the domain field of the 'Transit' default log type in backend/src/data/defaultLogTypes.json from 'personal' to 'work'. The seed file is read at server startup via seedDefaultLogTypes() which uses bulkWrite with $setOnInsert, so existing Transit records in the DB are not overwritten automatically — a one-time manual update or DB re-seed is needed to apply the change to already-seeded environments.
enhancements.notes: Because seedDefaultLogTypes uses $setOnInsert, this change only affects fresh environments or new deployments. To update an existing DB, run: db.defaultlogtypes.updateOne({ name: 'Transit' }, { $set: { domain: 'work' } }) in the MongoDB Atlas shell.

enhancements.title: Login screen: background image, large brand name, glass-morphism card
enhancements.description: Redesigned the login screen with three visual changes. (1) Background image: login_background.jpeg added to src/assets/ and applied to .login-page via background-image with a dual-tone brand gradient overlay (navy #15173D 72% → purple #982598 45%, 135deg). Used /assets/ absolute path in component inline styles (Angular does not process url() paths in styles:[...] through the build pipeline). (2) Product name moved outside the card as a standalone hero heading using Archivo Black (400, 80px) from Google Fonts — added to index.html link tag alongside Inter. Text colour #F1E9E9 with text-shadow for legibility over the background. Scales to 52px on mobile (≤480px). (3) Logo block removed from the login card. Card now starts directly with the tab switcher. Card background changed to rgba(18,19,32,0.82) + backdrop-filter:blur(18px) for glass-morphism effect.
enhancements.notes: Archivo Black is weight 400 by design — it is a display typeface with a single weight. Google Fonts URL: family=Archivo+Black. The /assets/ leading slash is required for Angular inline component styles because the Angular build does not rewrite url() references in styles:[...] arrays, unlike styleUrls which go through the CSS loader.

enhancements.title: Brand colour scheme: navy header, purple nav, navy timeline
enhancements.description: Applied a consistent brand colour palette across three UI zones via CSS custom properties in styles.css — changes apply to both dark and light themes. (1) Top nav bar: --header-bg #15173D (deep navy), --header-text #F1E9E9 (warm off-white), icon hover tints updated to rgba(241,233,233,...). (2) Left main menu: --nav-bg #982598 (purple), --nav-text #F1E9E9, --nav-text-muted rgba(241,233,233,0.55), --nav-item-hover rgba(255,255,255,0.12), --nav-item-active rgba(255,255,255,0.18), --nav-item-active-border #F1E9E9. (3) Timeline canvas: --timeline-bg #15173D, --timeline-text #F1E9E9, --timeline-text-muted rgba(241,233,233,0.4), --timeline-line #2A2D52.
enhancements.notes: All colour changes are in CSS custom properties only (styles.css :root and [data-theme=light]). No component template or logic was touched. Both themes use the same brand colours for header/nav/timeline since these are structural brand elements independent of light/dark preference.

enhancements.title: Log list under timeline with focus navigation
enhancements.description: Added a scrollable log list section below the timeline showing all entries for the selected day. Clicking any row highlights the corresponding bar in the timeline and smoothly scrolls it into view. Includes shimmer skeleton loader and empty state.

enhancements.title: Dark / Light mode toggle
enhancements.description: Added a sun/moon icon toggle in the top header to switch between dark and light themes. Theme preference is persisted in localStorage and restored on next visit. Light theme uses a clean white/grey palette.

enhancements.title: Left side navigation menu with Logger section
enhancements.description: Restructured the entire app shell to include a 210px left navigation panel. Added a 'Logger' nav item containing the calendar, timeline, and log list. The nav collapses to icon-only on narrow screens. Designed to support additional sections in the future.

enhancements.title: Double timeline view height
enhancements.description: Increased the Gantt timeline track height from 90px to 200px. Added min-height: calc(50vh - 80px) to the timeline container so it always occupies at least half the viewport. Log bars, drag overlays, and quarter-lines scale automatically.

enhancements.title: Deferred log form — open only on Create Log click
enhancements.description: Previously the create-log form opened immediately after a drag selection. Now it only opens when the user explicitly clicks the 'Create Log' button in the selection bar. Introduced a separate createLogClicked output event on TimelineComponent.

enhancements.title: CSV report download for selected day
enhancements.description: Added a 'Download report of the Day' card below the calendar. Clicking 'Download CSV' generates and downloads a CSV file (renmito-YYYY-MM-DD.csv) with columns: Date, Start Time, End Time, Duration, Activity Type, Label. Button is disabled when no logs exist. Generated entirely in the browser — no server round-trip.
enhancements.notes: Future: support PDF and XLSX export formats.

enhancements.title: Rename app from TimeLogger to Renmito
enhancements.description: Updated the app name in the header logo, browser tab title (<title>), and all relevant references from 'TimeLogger' to 'Renmito'.

enhancements.title: Enhancement log file + notepad drawer UI
enhancements.description: Created enhancements.json in src/assets to track all requested changes with version, type, date, description, tags and future-ready fields. Added a notepad icon in the header; clicking it opens a right-side slide-in drawer that loads and displays all enhancements from the file.
enhancements.notes: Future fields available in _schema: priority, requestedBy, breaking, timeSpentMins, pullRequest, branch, screenshot, changelog.

enhancements.title: Timeline hover time indicator + drag live end-time pill
enhancements.description: When hovering over the timeline (idle), a dashed vertical line and a time pill appear at the cursor showing the exact time at minute precision. During drag, a start-time anchor pill appears at the drag origin and a live blue end-time pill follows the cursor showing the current drag end time in real time. Both pills flip direction at the right edge to avoid overflow.

enhancements.title: Brand colour theme — orange header, navy nav, BFC9D1 sections
enhancements.description: Applied a new brand colour palette: top nav bar #E76F2E (orange), left side menu #25343F (dark navy), section backgrounds #BFC9D1, timeline default slot selection #BFC9D1, timeline background #25343F. Dark mode uses harmonious darker complements: header stays orange, nav #1A272F, sections #1E2E38, timeline #1A272F. Introduced --header-bg, --header-text, --header-icon-bg, --nav-text, --nav-text-muted, --timeline-text, --timeline-text-muted CSS variables to ensure correct contrast on always-dark nav and timeline regardless of theme.

enhancements.title: Log form activity chips + dropdown + custom type
enhancements.description: Redesigned the 'New Log Entry' form activity selector. Five common types (Work Log, Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner, Transit) are shown as quick-select chips with colour coding. Less-frequent types (Sleep, Exercise, Entertainment) appear in a dropdown. A '✏️ Custom' dropdown option reveals a free-text input so users can define any activity name. Edit mode correctly restores chip/dropdown/custom state. Legacy types retained in constants for backward compatibility.

enhancements.title: New brand colour palette — navy header, amber nav, cream sections, red timeline
enhancements.description: Applied a new colour palette: header #003049 (dark navy), left nav #fcbf49 (amber/yellow — requires dark nav text, a flip from previous always-light-on-dark-nav approach), section backgrounds #eae2b7 (warm cream), drag-selection overlay based on #f77f00 (orange), timeline background #d62828 (red). Dark mode uses harmonious darker complements: header #001F30, nav #C49030, surfaces #1E1C0E, timeline #8B1414. Updated --nav-text and --nav-text-muted to dark values for both themes since the nav is now a light amber surface. Fixed hardcoded #E76F2E and #ffffff nav colours in app.component.ts to use CSS variables.
enhancements.notes: Nav text is now dark (#1A1808 light / #1A1205 dark) because the amber nav background requires it for contrast. This inverts the previous always-dark-nav pattern from 1.10.

enhancements.title: Colour palette — charcoal header, steel-blue nav, white sections, coral timeline
enhancements.description: Applied a new colour palette: header #2b2d42 (dark charcoal), left nav #8d99ae (steel blue — medium luminance requires dark nav text), section backgrounds #FFFFFF (pure white), drag-selection overlay #e94f37 (coral red), timeline background #2b2d42 (same charcoal as header). Dark mode complements: header #1A1B28, nav #4A5568 (darker steel blue — now needs light text, flip from light mode), surfaces #1C1E30, timeline #1A1B28. Active nav accent is coral #e94f37 in dark mode and charcoal #2b2d42 in light mode. All nav text and active/hover colours are CSS-variable-driven for automatic adaptation.
enhancements.notes: Nav text direction flips between themes: light mode nav #8d99ae (medium) needs dark text #1A1C2A; dark mode nav #4A5568 (dark) needs light text #D0D8E8.

enhancements.title: Git repository setup and .gitignore data exclusion
enhancements.description: Initialised a GitHub repository at github.com/yatheendrasai1/renmito and pushed the codebase. Updated .gitignore to exclude runtime-generated filesystem log data (backend/data/*.json), build artefacts (frontend/dist/, frontend/.angular/), environment files (.env), and OS noise (.DS_Store). Untracked the previously committed data file so it no longer appears in version history going forward.
enhancements.notes: backend/data/ is intentionally excluded — it holds one JSON file per calendar day (YYYY-MM-DD.json) written at runtime by the Express API. These are user-specific logs and should not be committed.

enhancements.title: Timeline drag snaps to 10-minute intervals (A/B zone logic)
enhancements.description: Drag selection on the timeline now snaps in 10-minute increments in real time. Each 10-minute slot is divided into A-zone (0–4 min past the mark) and B-zone (5–9 min). In A-zone the selection boundary stays at the lower mark; once the cursor crosses the midpoint into B-zone it jumps to the next 10-min mark. Applies identically when dragging backward. Both the drag overlay and the live end-time pill update in 10-min steps as the cursor moves. Hover pill (non-drag) remains exact to the minute. Minimum selection reduced from 15 min to 10 min.
enhancements.notes: Implemented via Math.round(minutes/10)*10 applied live in onMouseDown, onTrackMouseMove, and onDocMouseMove. snapToQuarter(15) replaced with snapToTen(10). minutesToPixels() helper added.

enhancements.title: Editable start/end times in the log form
enhancements.description: Replaced the static time display in the New/Edit Log Entry popup with two native <input type='time'> fields. Both start and end times are now editable. The duration badge updates live as times change. The Save button is disabled when end time <= start time, with an inline 'end ≤ start' error indicator. In edit mode, the form is pre-populated with the existing log's times. ngOnChanges now also re-initialises on startTime/endTime input changes so time edits from the log list are reflected correctly.
enhancements.notes: formStartTime and formEndTime are internal state initialised from @Input startTime/endTime (create) or editEntry.startTime/endTime (edit). save() uses these form values, not the @Input props.

enhancements.title: MongoDB Atlas storage, JWT auth, two-collection log types, and DB-driven log form
enhancements.description: Full MongoDB Atlas integration replacing the filesystem JSON store. (1) Models: User (users — userName, email, passwordHash, creationType, timezone, isActive), DefaultLogType (defaultlogtypes — shared, read-only, account-agnostic; seeded once on server startup via seedDefaults.js), LogType (logtypes — user-scoped; userId, name, domain enum [work/personal/family], category, color, icon, isBuiltIn, isActive; renamed mode→domain, added category), TimeLog (timelogs — userId, logTypeId [refPath→logTypeSource], logTypeSource [DefaultLogType|LogType], title, description, startAt, endAt, status, durationMins, tags, source, lastHeartbeatAt; type and color fields removed entirely — name/color resolved at read-time via Mongoose populate). (2) Auth: POST /api/auth/signup creates a User with bcrypt-hashed password and returns a 7-day JWT. POST /api/auth/login verifies credentials and returns a JWT. authMiddleware.js verifies Bearer token on all protected routes. Centralised config.js owns the dotenv.config() call and exports db, auth, and server config keys for use across the backend. (3) Log type architecture: defaultlogtypes collection holds 15 built-in types seeded once (Code Time, Meeting, Design, Documentation, Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner, Food Intake, Transit, WellBeing, Zleep, Learning, Entertainment, Sports, Family Time) across work/personal/family domains. GET /api/logtypes merges both collections with a source tag (default|user). POST /api/logtypes creates a user-scoped type. validateLogTypeId() in logs.js checks DefaultLogType first then LogType and stores both the id reference and the source discriminator. (4) Frontend: LoginComponent (standalone full-page card, Log In/Sign Up tabs, password toggle, spinner, error banner). AuthService persists token+user in localStorage. Functional authInterceptor (HttpInterceptorFn) attaches Authorization: Bearer header to all non-auth requests. app.component.ts gates the main app behind authentication; username chip and logout button in header; logout clears auth + logtype cache. Log form popup fully DB-driven: chips grouped by domain (Work/Personal/Family), user types show ★ badge, shareReplay(1) cache on LogTypeService with tap()-based invalidation after mutations. Inline '+ New log type' toggle expands a Name + Domain + Color form that calls POST /api/logtypes, auto-selects the created type, and rebuilds chip groups. logTypeId is always required on save — no free-form type fallback.
enhancements.notes: Two-collection log type architecture uses Mongoose refPath on logTypeId + logTypeSource discriminator for dynamic populate. backend/data/ directory is now fully unused. DB_USERNAME, DB_PASSWORD, and JWT_SECRET must be set in backend/.env before starting the server. config.js is the single source of truth for all environment variables.

enhancements.title: Timeline UX polish: scrollbar corners, persistent create bar, levelled section headers
enhancements.description: Five UX fixes across the timeline and layout. (1) Scrollbar corner fix: added ::-webkit-scrollbar rules on .scroll-container so the scrollbar rail respects the container's border-radius, eliminating the sharp vs rounded edge inconsistency on mobile. (2) Removed redundant date label from the timeline header. (3) The selection badge ('+ Create Log' row) was conditionally rendered only when a drag selection existed, causing layout shift. It is now always visible (ngIf reduced to !isDragging). A default 1-hour selection is initialised on every date change via initDefaultSelection(): for today it uses the last 60 minutes snapped to 10-min grid; for other dates it defaults to 09:00–10:00. clearSelection() delegates to initDefaultSelection() so the bar never disappears. (4) Nav observation fix: .left-nav--collapsed now sets width:0, padding:0, border:0, overflow:hidden — fully invisible and space-free when closed. The @media (max-width:960px) block that was unconditionally forcing icon-only width was removed; the hamburger toggle is now the sole controller of open/closed state on all screen sizes. (5) Section header alignment: 'Logs for the day' heading and count badge moved out of log-list-section card into a content-header div above it, mirroring the left column's structure exactly. Both 'Timeline' and 'Logs for the day' labels now share the same content-header element and sit at the same visual height. log-list-header div and its CSS removed.
enhancements.notes: nav collapsed fix: localStorage key 'renmito-nav-collapsed' is read with !== 'false' so default is collapsed. Removing the 960px media query was key — it was overriding the navCollapsed class binding and preventing expand on any device narrower than 960px. Section header levelling: both columns now use content-header (outside the card) → card structure, making vertical alignment automatic via the CSS grid row.

enhancements.title: Touch drag support on mobile via Pointer Events API
enhancements.description: Replaced all mouse-specific event handlers on the timeline canvas (mousedown, mousemove, mouseenter, mouseleave) and the two document-level @HostListener decorators (document:mousemove, document:mouseup) with a single unified Pointer Events API implementation. onPointerDown calls setPointerCapture(event.pointerId) so pointermove and pointerup continue firing on the element even when the finger slides outside the canvas boundary, eliminating the need for document-level listeners. touch-action:none was added to .timeline-canvas so the browser does not intercept vertical touch movement for page scroll during a drag. Hover indicator and isHoveringTrack guard against pointerType === 'mouse' so the dashed hover line only appears for mouse users. pointercancel is mapped to onPointerUp to handle interrupted gestures (e.g. phone call, notification). HostListener removed from Angular imports entirely.
enhancements.notes: Key API: element.setPointerCapture(pointerId) replaces document-level mousemove/mouseup listeners. touch-action:none on the canvas is mandatory — without it the browser claims the touch gesture for scroll before pointerdown fires. Hover pill is suppressed for touch/pen pointerType to avoid visual noise during drag.

enhancements.title: Side-by-side timeline and log list (50/50 split)
enhancements.description: Restructured the logger content area from a single vertical stack to a two-column CSS grid. Left column (50%) contains the timeline header, timeline canvas, and drag hint. Right column (50%) contains the log list. The log list column is position:sticky top:0 and has max-height:638px with overflow-y:auto so it scrolls independently at the same visual height as the timeline. On mobile (≤700px) the grid collapses to a single column — timeline full-width on top, log list full-width below with no max-height constraint.
enhancements.notes: 638px max-height on the log list matches the approximate rendered height of the timeline column (content-header ~30px + gap 14px + timeline-container 552px + gap 14px + hint 24px). Responsive breakpoint at 700px (narrower than the nav breakpoint at 960px).

enhancements.title: Compact date bar with calendar popup replacing always-open calendar panel
enhancements.description: Removed the always-visible left-column calendar panel and the separate 'Download report of the Day' card. Replaced them with a compact date bar at the top of the content area showing the selected date, a calendar icon, and a download icon. Clicking the calendar icon opens a centered overlay popup containing the CalendarComponent with Cancel and Apply buttons at the bottom. Selecting a date inside the popup updates a pendingDate without loading logs — logs are only fetched when Apply is clicked (or popup closed via Cancel/overlay click). Clicking the download icon triggers the CSV download directly. The logger layout is now full-width single column (no more 300px/1fr grid split).
enhancements.notes: pendingDate is a clone of selectedDate when the popup opens. onPendingDateSelected() updates pendingDate on calendar click. applyPendingDate() copies pendingDate → selectedDate and triggers loadLogs(). Overlay click-outside closes without applying.

enhancements.title: Collapsible left navigation with hamburger toggle
enhancements.description: Added a hamburger button in the top-left header. Clicking it toggles the left nav between expanded (210px, icons + labels) and collapsed (52px, icons only) states. Collapse state is persisted in localStorage under 'renmito-nav-collapsed' and restored on page load. The nav animates with a 0.22s ease width transition. In collapsed mode, nav group labels and item text are hidden, and items are centre-aligned. The collapsed button item shows a title tooltip for accessibility.
enhancements.notes: Uses .left-nav--collapsed class bound via [class.left-nav--collapsed]='navCollapsed'. CSS transition on width gives a smooth slide. Complements the existing @media (max-width: 960px) responsive collapse.

enhancements.title: Enhancement log migrated to MongoDB; UI drawer removed
enhancements.description: Created an Enhancement Mongoose model (enhancements collection) with fields: id, version, type, title, description, status, implementedAt, tags, relatedTo, requestedBy, breaking, notes. Added seedEnhancements.js utility that reads enhancements.json and upserts all entries using bulkWrite with $setOnInsert — existing documents are never overwritten and new entries added to the JSON file are picked up on the next server start. Added GET /api/enhancements and POST /api/enhancements routes (auth-protected). seedEnhancements() is called alongside seedDefaultLogTypes() inside ensureConnection() in app.js. Removed the EnhancementsDrawerComponent from app.component.ts (imports array, template usage, showEnhancementsDrawer property, and the notepad header button). The notepad drawer UI is fully gone.
enhancements.notes: seedEnhancements reads from frontend/src/assets/enhancements.json via path.resolve. Vercel's nft bundler traces and includes this file automatically. Going forward, add new enhancement entries to enhancements.json — they will be upserted on next cold start.

enhancements.title: Vercel deployment — frontend static site + serverless API
enhancements.description: Configured the full-stack app for zero-config Vercel deployment as a single project. (1) vercel.json at the root sets installCommand (backend npm install), buildCommand (Angular production build), outputDirectory (frontend/dist/renmito-frontend/browser), and two rewrites: /api/:path* → /api/index (serverless function), /:path* → /index.html (SPA fallback). (2) api/index.js at root is the Vercel serverless entry point — exports the Express app so Vercel can invoke it as an HTTP handler. (3) backend/src/app.js refactored for serverless: mongoose.connect() is now lazy and cached per container (ensureConnection() with readyState check and promise caching), preventing connection leaks on cold starts; app.listen() is conditional on !process.env.VERCEL so local dev still works normally; CORS origin is now read from config.cors.origin instead of hardcoded. (4) backend/src/config.js gained a cors.origin key defaulting to http://localhost:4200, overridable via CORS_ORIGIN env var (set to * in Vercel dashboard). (5) Angular environments: created frontend/src/environments/environment.ts (apiBase: http://localhost:5890/api) and environment.prod.ts (apiBase: /api — relative, so production calls go to the same Vercel domain). angular.json production config now has fileReplacements to swap environment.ts with environment.prod.ts at build time. (6) All three frontend services (LogService, LogTypeService, AuthService) now derive their apiBase from environment.apiBase instead of hardcoded localhost:5890. (7) backend/.env.example updated with CORS_ORIGIN documentation.
enhancements.notes: Vercel env vars to set in dashboard: DB_USERNAME, DB_PASSWORD, JWT_SECRET, CORS_ORIGIN=*. No VERCEL env var needed — Vercel injects it automatically. Local dev unchanged: backend runs on port 5890, frontend on port 4200.

enhancements.title: Remove legacy field mapping — reflect timelogs collection directly
enhancements.description: Removed the toFrontend() workaround function from logs.js that mapped timelogs collection field names to the old UI structure. The API response now directly mirrors the timelogs collection: startAt/endAt (HH:MM, matching DB field names) instead of startTime/endTime; title instead of label; a nested logType object { id, name, color, domain, category } instead of flat type and color strings; durationMins included. POST/PUT body now accepts title instead of label. Frontend LogEntry model updated to match: startAt/endAt, title, logType nested object, durationMins — legacy fields label/type/color removed. CreateLogEntry uses title instead of label. Timeline component updated to use log.startAt/endAt, log.title, log.logType.color. LogFormComponent initForm() now reads editEntry.startAt/endAt, editEntry.title, editEntry.logType.id/name. AppComponent log list renders log.title and log.logType.name directly — no lookup function. getActivityLabel() removed entirely. activity-types.ts constants file deleted — it was a hardcoded lookup of old type keys (work, breakfast, lunch…) that returned wrong labels for DB-backed type names. downloadCSV() uses log.startAt, log.endAt, log.logType.name, log.title.
enhancements.notes: activity-types.ts (ACTIVITY_TYPES constant array, getActivityColor, getActivityLabel) is fully deleted. The only remaining time-string conversion is toTimeStr() in logs.js which formats UTC Date objects to HH:MM for the API response — this is a legitimate serialisation concern, not a field-name workaround.

enhancements.title: Vertical timeline with logs displayed to the right
enhancements.description: Complete redesign of the timeline from a horizontal 1920px scrolling strip to a vertical 1440px (24h × 60px/hr) scrollable canvas. Time labels sit in the left column (0–46px). A narrow drag strip (46–70px) with subtle background is the interaction zone — clicking/dragging outside it (on the log area) does not start a selection. Log bars are vertical bands (left: 74px to right edge) positioned at their exact start/end times since 1px = 1min. The selection info (time range, duration, + Create Log button, clear button) has moved from the bottom selection bar into the timeline header row beside the date label. Drag snapping (10-min A/B zones from 1.15) is preserved. Hover dashed line is now horizontal. All time pills are positioned to the right of the drag strip so they don't overlap hour labels.
enhancements.notes: HOUR_HEIGHT=60, TOTAL_HEIGHT=1440=TOTAL_MINUTES so pixelsToMinutes and minutesToPixels are 1:1. Pre-computed tickMarks array (120 entries) used instead of nested ngFor. LOG_AREA_OFFSET=74 guard prevents drag from starting when clicking log bars.
